name: Can I just go see my own doctor if the workers' comp doctor won't help me?

mainEntity.name: Can I just go see my own doctor if the workers' comp doctor won't help me?

acceptedAnswer.text: Be careful. If you see your own doctor without insurance authorization, they may refuse to pay the bill. You have the right to request a change of doctor, but you must follow specific legal steps to ensure the bills are covered.
acceptedAnswer.description:
If you have an emergency, go to the ER. However, for non-emergency care, going to your own 'private' doctor without authorization can result in the insurance company refusing to pay those specific bills. This is a common trap. You often need to 'petition' the state to change doctors if the company doctor is refusing treatment.

The main factor is whether the treatment was an 'authorized' referral or an 'unauthorized' self-referral.

If you already went to your own doctor, keep the bills but do not pay them out of pocket yet. Your attorney can often get them 'ratified' or paid retroactively if the care was necessary.

Navigating the 'authorized doctor' maze in Utah County is difficult; we help clients switch to doctors who actually care about their recovery.
